hints: Start with the Priority enum. Import Enum from the enum module, then define 'class Priority(Enum):' with three members: LOW = 'low', MEDIUM = 'medium', HIGH = 'high'.
For the Task dataclass, add the @dataclass decorator above the class definition. This generates __init__, __repr__, and __eq__ automatically from the annotated fields.

The tags field needs 'field(default_factory=list)' as its default, not '= []'. Writing '= []' would share the same list across all Task instances — the decorator actually raises an error to prevent this.
In summary(), get the priority label with 'self.priority.value.upper()'. For example, Priority.HIGH.value is 'high', and .upper() turns it into 'HIGH'.
For the tags part of summary(), use a conditional expression: tag_str = ', '.join(self.tags) if self.tags else 'none'. An empty list is falsy, so 'if self.tags' is False when there are no tags.
